evidence.claim: ENERGY STAR certified dishwashers use on average 12% less energy and 30% less water than standard models; Consumer Reports verifies this in independent testing.
Decibel ratings for dishwashers are measured at 1 meter under specific test conditions; ratings below 44 dBA are generally perceived as 'quiet' in open-plan kitchens.

criteria.name: noise_dba
criteria.notes: <44 dBA is quiet for open-plan (E2). <40 is library-quiet.

criteria.name: cycle_time_minutes
criteria.notes: Quieter and more efficient cycles run longer.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Not standardized. Actual dBA rating must be stated and compared.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is a specific dBA figure cited?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Drying performance varies sharply by brand, cycle, and detergent/rinse aid use. Bosch and Miele drying is better than typical; LG and some Whirlpool models struggle with plastics.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the drying claim brand-specific, cycle-specific, or generic marketing?

compatibilityQuestions: Do you have a garbage disposal? Some dishwashers require a disposal or a filter; others have built-in food grinders.
Is your kitchen open-plan? If yes, prioritize ≤42 dBA.

repairability.notes: Parts readily available; most repairs under $300 are economically justified.
